license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.64ghgtw
title: Long-read DNA preparation for bacterial isolates.
author: He Sun

steps_raw.instruction: Add cation-adjusted Müller-Hinton broth in a 15 ml Falcon tube. (Müller-Hinton medium is used here for antibiotic-resistant bacteria) 5 mL, CAMHB
Add 1/4 10 µl-loop of colony from an agar plate in the solution.
Vortex the mixture for 5 s at maximum speed.
Incubate the mixture overnight on a shaking bench at the speed of 120 rpm at 37 ℃. 37 °C
Add 2 ml of the incubated mixture in a 2.5 ml Eppendorf tube.
Centrifuge the mixture. 20 °C 00:10:00
Resuspend the bacterial pellet in Buffer G3 by vortexing. 1 mL, Buffer G3
Add lysozyme and Proteinase K 20 µL, lysozyme (100 mg/ml) 25 µL, Proteinase K
Gently resuspend the mixture and incubate at 37 ℃ for 20 min without shaking. 37 °C 00:20:00
Add Buffer G4 400 µL, Buffer G4
Gently resuspend the mixture and incubate at 50 ℃ for 30 min without shaking. 50 °C 00:30:00
Centrifuge the mixture. 20 °C 00:05:00
Equilibrate the column (AXG 20) with Buffer N2. 1 mL, Buffer N2
Add Buffer N2 (room temperature) to the sample. Vortex the mixture for 15 s at maximum speed. 1 mL, Buffer N2
Load 1 ml of the mixture on the column. Allow it to enter the resin by gravity flow.
Discard the supernatant. (For anaerobes, due to low concentration of bacterial culture, it is suggested to repeat step 5 to step 7 several times in order to obtain enough amount of DNA)
Reload 1 ml of the mixture on the column. Do not reload if the mixture of former step has not been filtered completely in case the column gets clogged.
Reload the rest mixture.
Transfer the supernatant to a 5 ml Eppendorf tube.
Wash the column with Buffer N3. 1 mL, Buffer N3 three times

Elute the genomic DNA with Buffer N5. Collect the DNA solution in a 2.5 ml Eppendorf tube. 1 mL, Buffer N5 The average concentration of DNA is approximately 10 µg/ml. Store at 4 ℃ if you want to stop here 4 °C, Do not freeze
Add magnetic Beads ( 0.35 volume, AMPure) to the DNA sample. Gently resuspend by hand. 350 µL, Magnetic beads 1000 µL, DNA sample
Incubate the mixture. 00:10:00 20 °C, Room temperature
Spin down and let the beads bind to magnetic plate until the liquid is clear.
Stay on the magnet and remove supernatant.
Wash with Ethanol. Do not disturb pellet. 150 µL, Ethanol (80%)
Remove supernatant.
go to step #26
Spin down and let the beads bind to magnetic plate again. Remove the residual liquid with a 10 µl pipette.
Add nuclease free water and resuspend gently. 55 µL, Nuclease free water
Incubate the DNA solution. 00:15:00 37 °C
Spin down and put on the magnetic plate. Retrieve the supernatant (DNA) with a 10 µl pipette. Determine dsDNA concentration e.g. by Qubit. Determine DNA length by Agarose gel or Bioanalyzer.
